summary: The delivery has broad green regression evidence, but independent review found false-positive paths in held-out evaluation, claim grounding, runtime history, genome lifecycle cleanup, path containment, specialist execution, and playbook provenance.

findings.description: Manifest-authored numeric dimensions can produce a held-out PASS without executing a task, and the genome comparison does not enforce a controlled with-versus-without binding run.

evidence.detail: caseDimensions returns testCase.dimensions before considering worker output; evaluateHeldOutCase accepts this as the complete case.
The default PASS fixture declares static dimensions and no held-out worker. The A/B fixture uses two unrelated worker scripts.

findings.impact: A persistent or regulated squad can satisfy the premium gate with self-authored scores instead of unseen executable evidence.
findings.recommendation: Require executable candidate evidence for every held-out PASS and a same-task controlled genome toggle for A/B. Treat static dimensions as supplemental only.
alternatives: If no executable evaluator exists, return UNVERIFIED and keep the squad non-ready.
findings.residual_risk: Judge calibration can remain imperfect, but it must not be replaced by manifest assertions.

findings.description: Evidence Pack construction implicitly assigns all collected sources to an unmapped claim and labels it supported; a live pack can also pass with no claims.

evidence.detail: Missing claim.source_ids falls back to every source ID and supported status.
Live status is based on source count and domain independence, not claim support.

findings.impact: The promised source-to-claim trace may be fabricated by structure even when no source semantically supports the claim.
findings.recommendation: Default unmapped claims to unverified, validate explicit source IDs, and block material-claim readiness when claims are empty, contradicted, or unsupported.
alternatives: Allow a source-discovery-only pack to pass only when no material claim contract is declared.
findings.residual_risk: Semantic support still depends on the selected evaluator, so the report must preserve unverified states.

findings.description: Retry exhaustion overwrites attempt history and records completed_at on escalation; a capability-gap specialist is ignored by the legacy autorun engine.

evidence.detail: runTaskWithGapClosure replaces task.result with a short escalation object and sets completed_at. runTask selects task.executor rather than task.specialist.
Current tests disable gap closure and do not cover specialist execution.

findings.impact: AC-premium-13 loses the causal trail, while AC-premium-07 can silently execute specialized work with the wrong actor.
findings.recommendation: Merge the escalation result into existing history, keep completed_at null, and fail or escalate when the active engine cannot execute a required specialist.
alternatives: Materialize a session-scoped worker for the specialist when the legacy engine supports it.
findings.residual_risk: Parallel voting writes remain a separate legacy concern outside this correction.

findings.description: A removed, stale, conflicted, or no-effect genome leaves its previously compiled managed block and checklist active.

evidence.detail: materializeExecutorEffects returns without touching files when compiled.length is zero.

findings.impact: Manifest readiness and actual executor behavior diverge; an operator can remove a genome while its method continues influencing output.
findings.recommendation: Remove or explicitly deactivate the prior managed block/checklist whenever no current compiled binding applies, with lifecycle regression tests.
alternatives: Keep an inactive audit file, but never leave old instructions in the executable prompt.
findings.residual_risk: Multi-file atomicity remains best-effort unless a transaction journal is added later.

findings.description: New squad paths accept unchecked slugs, live freshness uses a seven-day default inconsistent with six-hour and 24-hour policies, and playbook reinforcement overwrites the original origin.

evidence.detail: The CLI slug is joined directly into report read/write paths without canonical validation.
Live evidence defaults to maxAgeDays 7 and does not require policy identity.
Reinforcement assigns entry.from from the latest observation.

findings.impact: Path containment can be bypassed, stale evidence can be credited as current, and learned-rule provenance can be rewritten.
findings.recommendation: Validate squad slugs before path use, apply the strictest policy-derived age, reject policy mismatch, preserve immutable origin and use atomic playbook writes.
findings.residual_risk: DNS rebinding defense remains a lower-level networking hardening candidate.

evidence.detail: The PRD requires real held-out execution and claim-level provenance.
Static manifest scores can satisfy the held-out verdict.

specification_fidelity.residual_risk: Required premium promises are not yet true on all production paths.

evidence.detail: 20 of 20 AC IDs have assertions, but independent review found missing behavioral cases inside those ACs.

acceptance_coverage.residual_risk: Coverage mapping is complete, while negative-path behavioral coverage is incomplete.

evidence.detail: Lifecycle cleanup does not remove stale managed instructions.
Retry exhaustion replaces rather than extends the durable attempt record.

code_health.residual_risk: State transitions can diverge from persisted behavior.

evidence.detail: The passing held-out fixture executes no held-out worker.
Legacy execution bypasses a required ephemeral specialist.

runtime_truth.residual_risk: A ready state can still be reached without the promised execution.

evidence.detail: Unchecked squad slug reaches filesystem writes.
Freshness enforcement is weaker than the runtime policy.

residual_risk.residual_risk: Gate D must remain blocked until these bounded corrections are independently retested.
